title: register: prompt for missing username, fix 409 username_taken detection
description: Two bugs in links register. (1) No username in links.yaml → 422 because body has no username field. Fix: prompt interactively before calling the API. (2) 409 username_taken detection checks data.code === 'username_taken' but the API returns the string in data.message or data.error with no code/suggestions fields — so the conflict flow is never entered and it falls through to the generic error handler.

features: FIX 1 — prompt for username when not set in config:
In the action handler, after reading config, check if config.username is set and passes validateUsername(). If not, prompt the user: 'Username (3-30 chars, lowercase, hyphens ok): '
Validate the input with validateUsername(). If invalid, print the error and re-prompt in a loop until a valid username is entered.
Use a readline interface (same ask() helper pattern). Close it in a finally block.
Set body.username to the prompted value before the first API call.
Do NOT prompt if config.username is already set and valid — use it directly.
The current check is: response.status 4xx AND data.code === 'username_taken' AND Array.isArray(data.suggestions)
Change to detect username_taken if: response.status === 409 OR (any 4xx AND (data.code === 'username_taken' OR data.message === 'username_taken' OR data.error === 'username_taken'))

The suggestions array may not always be present — handle missing/empty suggestions gracefully: if data.suggestions is not an array or is empty, pass an empty array [] to resolveUsername() so the UI still works (user will just see only the 'Enter a different username' option)
In resolveUsername(): if currentSuggestions is empty, skip the 'Suggestions:' section entirely and go straight to the 'Enter a different username' prompt without listing numbered suggestions

Also update validateConfig() in src/config.js to accept an optional 'username' string field (same validation as other optional string fields — must be a string if present, no further format validation needed at config level since format is enforced at registration time)

notes: For FIX 1: the username prompt readline interface can be the same one reused by resolveUsername() if it gets called — but to keep it simple, open a fresh rl for the initial prompt and close it before the API call. resolveUsername() opens its own rl internally. For FIX 2: the real-world API may never send suggestions — the CLI must work correctly even when suggestions: [] or suggestions is absent from the 409 response.
